name: Pessons Lakes Circuit
name_original: Estanques de Pessons — circular desde Grau Roig
description: Jog the difficult Estanques de Pessons circular route from Grau Roig, a 4.9-mile trail with 1297 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging in the Pyrenees offers diverse terrain, from rolling green hillsides and dense forests to high valleys and granite-laden regions. The mountain range features numerous peaks, crystal-clear lakes, and impressive waterfalls, creating varied running backdrops. Glacial cirques and extensive trail networks characterize the landscape, providing options for all levels of runners. The region also includes coastal paths overlooking the Atlantic Ocean, adding another dimension to its running environments. Best jogging routes in the Pyrenees The most popular jogging route is Classic loop – running to the Rhune from Olhette, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain towards the Rhune. Another top favourite among local runners is Loop via Ziburu Mendi – running to the Rhune from Olhette, a difficult 7.3 miles (11.8 km) path. This trail navigates through the varied terrain surrounding the Rhune, providing challenging ascents. Local runners also love the SENPEREKO Trail – running in the Basque Country, a 13.1 miles (21.1 km) trail leading through the diverse landscapes of the Basque Country, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Jogging in the Pyrenees is defined by mountain peaks, glacial cirques, and coastal paths. The extensive network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate trails to difficult mountain runs. The routes in Pyrenees are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2400 reviews. More than 205000 runners have used komoot to explore Pyrenees's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Pyrenees?
A: The Pyrenees offer a vast network of running routes, with over 9,800 trails available on komoot. This includes a wide range of options, from easy paths to challenging mountain runs, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of running routes in the Pyrenees?
A: The Pyrenees provide a diverse range of running experiences. You'll find approximately 400 easy routes, over 3,700 moderate trails, and more than 5,600 difficult runs. This variety ensures options for beginners and experienced trail runners alike.

Q: What do other runners say about the routes in the Pyrenees?
A: The running routes in the Pyrenees are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 2,400 reviews. More than 205,000 runners have explored the region's varied terrain, often praising the stunning natural beauty and extensive trail networks.

Q: Are there running routes with waterfalls or lakes in the Pyrenees?
A: Yes, the Pyrenees are dotted with numerous crystal-clear lakes and impressive waterfalls, creating idyllic and scenic running backdrops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949461728/cola-de-caballo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cola de Caballo Trail&lt;/a&gt; in Ordesa y Monte Perdido National Park features stunning waterfalls and valley views. Other notable areas include Gaube Lake in Pyrenees National Park and the beautiful Lake Agalops in Néouvielle Nature Reserve.

Q: Can I find coastal running paths in the Pyrenees?
A: Absolutely. For a different running experience, the Basque Corniche offers an incredible coastal footpath overlooking the Atlantic Ocean. You can enjoy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e878711545/saint-jean-de-luz-and-socoa-coastal-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Jean-de-Luz and Socoa Coastal Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which winds along vast meadows, wild creeks, and jagged cliffs at the foot of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there routes that offer panoramic mountain views?
A: The Pyrenees are renowned for their breathtaking mountain views. Many routes ascend to peaks or traverse high valleys, offering extensive panoramas. Notable summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/885205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canigou&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/151545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Perdido&lt;/a&gt; provide stunning 360-degree vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008065782/loop-via-ziburu-mendi-running-to-the-rhune-from-olhette" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop via Ziburu Mendi – running to the Rhune from Olhette&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent option for challenging ascents and rewarding views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Pyrenees running trails?
A: The Pyrenees offer incredibly diverse terrain for runners. You can expect everything from rolling green hillsides and dense forests to high valleys, granite-laden regions, and even technical mountain trails with rocks and roots. This variety ensures that every run can be a unique adventure.

Q: Are there family-friendly running trails in the Pyrenees?
A: While many routes in the Pyrenees are challenging, there are also easier paths suitable for families. Look for routes with minimal elevation gain and shorter distances, often found in the lower valleys or around accessible lakes like Suyen Lake in the Azun Valley, which is known for family-friendly walks.

Q: Are dog-friendly running routes available in the Pyrenees?
A: Many trails in the Pyrenees are suitable for running with dogs, especially in regional parks and less restricted areas. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations, especially within national parks, regarding dogs on trails. Always keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of wildlife.

Q: Are there circular running routes in the Pyrenees?
A: Yes, the Pyrenees feature numerous circular running routes, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Many of the listed routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040736608/senpereko-trail-running-in-the-basque-country" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;SENPEREKO Trail – running in the Basque Country&lt;/a&gt;, are designed as loops, offering a complete experience without retracing your steps.

Q: What kind of gear is recommended for running in the Pyrenees mountains?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for rapid weather changes, it's wise to be prepared. Recommended gear includes appropriate trail running shoes with good grip, layers of clothing (even in summer), a waterproof jacket, sufficient water and snacks, a map or GPS device, and a fully charged phone. For more challenging routes, poles can also be beneficial.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions along the running routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, many running routes in the Pyrenees pass by or offer access to interesting landmarks. You might encounter charming villages, medieval castles, or natural highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1016283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rhune summit&lt;/a&gt;, which is a feature on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045708496/skyrhune-running-in-the-basque-country" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Skyrhune – running in the Basque Country&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts dramatic glacial cirques, such as Cirque de Gavarnie, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

Q: When is the best time of year for running in the Pyrenees?
A: The best time for running in the Pyrenees generally depends on the altitude and specific trails. Spring (May-June) and autumn (September-October) offer pleasant temperatures and stunning scenery, with fewer crowds. Summer (July-August) is ideal for higher-altitude trails, though afternoon thunderstorms can occur. Lower-elevation and coastal paths can be enjoyed year-round, with winter offering unique snow running opportunities in some areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Estanques de Pessons circular trail?
faqs.answer: For optimal safety and clear paths, the best time to visit is typically between late June and late September. Depending on conditions, the season can extend from May to October.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail officially starts from the Cubil car park in Grandvalira-Grau Roig. This car park offers convenient access to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is the Estanques de Pessons circular route suitable for jogging?
faqs.answer: While the Komoot tour is categorized for jogging, the trail is generally rated as moderate to difficult for hiking. Some sections near the lakes offer gentle slopes suitable for running, but other parts involve steep climbs, granite screes, and navigating boulders, making continuous jogging challenging. It's best suited for experienced trail runners accustomed to varied mountain terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies significantly. You'll start on a forest track, which then transitions to more rugged paths with granite screes and zig-zags. Expect to navigate boulders and streams in some areas. While generally well-marked with GR7 and GRP signage, some variant routes may have less discernible paths.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is renowned for its spectacular high-mountain landscape and numerous crystal-clear glacial lakes. You'll pass by highlights such as Pessons Lakes and Estany Primer, along with Estany Rodó, Meligar Lake, Les Fonts Lake, and Cap dels Pessons. The route also offers panoramic views of surrounding peaks and the largest glacial cirque in Andorra.

faqs.question: Is the Estanques de Pessons circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect the local wildlife, which includes marmots, cows, and horses, and to respect other trail users.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Estanques de Pessons circular trail.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the GRP La volta a Andorra and the GR 7 - Andorra.

faqs.question: Is there a place to eat or rest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a mountain hut restaurant at Estany Primer, which offers a convenient stop for refreshments and rest during your journey.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: As you explore the high-mountain environment, you might encounter various wildlife, including marmots, cows, and horses. The area is also home to diverse wildflowers during the warmer months.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the route?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour suggests a jogging duration of approximately 1 hour and 17 minutes. However, for hiking, especially given the varied and challenging terrain, it would take considerably longer, depending on your pace and stops.
